title: Parasenecio × cuneatus
taxonomy: Parasenecio × cuneatus is a hybrid plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the Senecio genus, which includes a wide range of plants commonly known as ragworts. The Senecio genus is further divided into several sections, with Parasenecio × cuneatus being a part of one of them. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics. The plant's hybrid nature and classification can be complex, with some sources grouping it differently.

benefits: Parasenecio × cuneatus, which is a hybrid plant, is primarily known for its ornamental value rather than its health benefits. constituents: Parasenecio × cuneatus contains a variety of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. intro: Parasenecio × cuneatus, also known as the hybrid creosote bush, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infections.
